what am i looking at? ↗

These are structures — like buildings, meant to protect from the elements. They attempt to protect and make a cozy interior for activities that are hard to do in the open. They don't try to lock in, they just are an invitation to come inside for awhile.

There is a tension that these are built with imperial factory time — scheduled, divided, measured. But they are not meant to force behavior. They are meant to create space for things I want to do and be doing. This creates time protected from the feeling that I "should be doing X" — and protected from an attention economy spending billions to keep us watching short form videos.

Visual design inspired by Anymals — a puzzle game by Galt Toys, designed by Ken Garland, with artwork by Daria Gan (1960s).

Anymals — Galt Toys, designed by Ken Garland, artwork by Daria Gan (1960s).

Colour palette from Klara and the Sun (Faber & Faber), cover design by Pete Adlington. The novel by Kazuo Ishiguro is narrated by Klara, a solar-powered Artificial Friend designed to provide companionship to children.

Klara and the Sun — Faber & Faber, cover design by Pete Adlington.

Pop-Up design from Anne Lee Steele

Site Designed by Sage Voorhees at Ferguson Lynch
